topic: Why most AI businesses fail in 90 days
hook_summary: The real reason your AI startup is dying is not the tech.

scenes.audio_text: I've watched 47 AI startups burn cash in 90 days. The ones that survived didn't have better tech.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium close-up of @daniel sitting on a concrete ledge in an industrial park, overcast natural light, locked-off camera, documentary style. He looks directly into lens with a slight smirk.

scenes.audio_text: They had someone who could sell the outcome, not the bot. One guy sold a lead gen agent to dental clinics for $2k a month — he didn't mention AI once.
scenes.visual_prompt: Over-the-shoulder shot of @daniel walking through a busy city street, candid, soft afternoon light, slight camera wobble as if handheld. He gestures with one hand while talking.

scenes.audio_text: Meanwhile, the technical founders were still arguing about the best vector database. Their product was better. But no one cared.
scenes.visual_prompt: Wide shot of @daniel sitting at a small table in a quiet cafe, natural window light, slow push-in. He leans forward, expression serious.

scenes.audio_text: AI is the easiest part. Selling is the moat. That's the whole thing.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium close-up of @daniel leaning against a brick wall, golden hour light, locked-off. He gives a slight nod after finishing his sentence.
